questions.q: Raigad Fort served as the capital of whose kingdom?
questions.explanation: Raigad Fort was the capital of Shivaji's Maratha kingdom.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Raigad Fort located?
questions.explanation: Raigad Fort stands in the Sahyadri range of present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: What type of fort is Raigad?
questions.explanation: Raigad is a hill fortress set high in the Sahyadri mountains.

questions.q: Which major event took place at Raigad Fort in 1674?
questions.explanation: Shivaji was crowned Chhatrapati at Raigad Fort in 1674.

questions.q: In which mountain range does Raigad Fort stand?
questions.explanation: Raigad lies in the Sahyadri range, the Western Ghats of Maharashtra.

questions.q: Raigad Fort remains closely tied to the identity of which people?
questions.explanation: Raigad is intimately associated with Maratha identity and pride.

questions.q: The title Shivaji took at his Raigad coronation was:
questions.explanation: At Raigad, Shivaji was crowned with the sovereign title Chhatrapati.

questions.q: Raigad Fort is reached by climbing a long flight of what?
questions.explanation: The fort is famously approached by a long, steep flight of stone steps (a ropeway now also exists).

questions.q: Raigad Fort was chosen by Shivaji largely because of its:
questions.explanation: Raigad's steep cliffs and commanding height gave it strong natural defences, ideal as a capital.

questions.q: Who is buried at a memorial on Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Shivaji died at Raigad in 1680 and his samadhi (memorial) stands on the fort.

questions.q: By what earlier name was Raigad known before Shivaji captured and renamed it?
questions.explanation: The hill was earlier called Rairi before Shivaji took it and renamed it Raigad.

questions.q: In which year did Shivaji die at Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Shivaji died at Raigad in 1680, six years after his coronation there.

questions.q: The grand entrance gate of Raigad Fort is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The principal gateway of Raigad is called the Maha Darwaja, the great gate.

questions.q: Which open-air assembly hall on Raigad, where the throne stood, hosted royal audiences?
questions.explanation: Raigad's royal court area, fronted by the Nagarkhana gateway, served as Shivaji's audience hall (Rajya Sabha).

questions.q: From which adviser did Shivaji acquire Raigad, the local Chandrarao More chiefs, around the mid-17th century?
questions.explanation: Shivaji gained the Rairi hill after subduing the Chandrarao More chiefs of Javli in the 1650s.

questions.q: Raigad Fort is sometimes called the 'Gibraltar of the East' for its:
questions.explanation: Raigad's commanding, cliff-girt position earned it the nickname 'Gibraltar of the East'.

questions.q: Which large water reservoir on Raigad supplied the fort's inhabitants?
questions.explanation: The Ganga Sagar reservoir on Raigad provided water for the fort's population.

questions.q: Which bastion-and-cliff features are well-known landmarks on Raigad Fort?
questions.explanation: Raigad features the Hirakani bastion and the Takmak Tok cliff, both well-known landmarks of the fort.

questions.q: Which Maratha king held a second coronation at Raigad in 1674, a few months after the first?
questions.explanation: Shivaji underwent a second tantric-rite coronation at Raigad later in 1674 after the first ceremony.

questions.q: After Shivaji's death, Raigad briefly fell into the hands of which empire in 1689?
questions.explanation: The Mughals under Aurangzeb captured Raigad in 1689 following the execution of Sambhaji.

questions.q: Raigad Fort is located in which district, named after the fort itself?
questions.explanation: The fort gives its name to Raigad district in Maharashtra's Konkan region.

questions.q: The market street built atop Raigad for traders is known as the:
questions.explanation: Raigad had a planned market street, the Bazaar Peth, lined with shop foundations still visible today.

questions.q: Which engineer-architect is traditionally credited with fortifying and laying out Raigad as Shivaji's capital?
questions.explanation: The architect Hiroji Indulkar is credited with designing and building the works of Raigad Fort.

questions.q: The cliff on Raigad from which condemned prisoners were said to be thrown is called:
questions.explanation: Takmak Tok is the sheer execution cliff at Raigad over which condemned prisoners were reputedly cast.

questions.q: The Hirakani Buruj bastion at Raigad is named after a woman who legendarily did what?
options: Led the fort's defence in battle
Climbed down the cliff at night to reach her child
questions.explanation: Legend holds that a milkmaid named Hirakani scaled down the steep cliff at night to reach her infant, inspiring the bastion's name.

questions.q: Which temple on Raigad Fort did Shivaji worship at, dedicated to a form of the goddess?
questions.explanation: Raigad has the Jagdishwar (Shiva) temple and the Shirkai goddess shrine, both linked to Shivaji's worship.

questions.q: Which English factor of the East India Company attended Shivaji's 1674 coronation at Raigad and left an account?
questions.explanation: Henry Oxinden of the English East India Company was present at Shivaji's Raigad coronation and recorded it.

questions.q: Roughly how high does Raigad Fort rise above the surrounding plain at its base?
questions.explanation: Raigad rises about 820 metres (some 2,700 feet) above the valley floor at its base, sitting at roughly 1,350 metres above sea level on an isolated Sahyadri hill.

questions.q: In the 19th century, who restored Shivaji's neglected samadhi memorial at Raigad?
options: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule (and later Tilak's committee)
questions.explanation: Jyotirao Phule drew attention to the neglected samadhi, and Bal Gangadhar Tilak later led efforts to restore it.

questions.q: Raigad's fortifications were notably strengthened after Shivaji captured the hill in which decade?
questions.explanation: Shivaji took the hill of Rairi in the 1650s and developed it through the 1660s-1670s into his fortified capital.
